perturbation.original_text: I will only access confidential or sensitive data for legitimate business purposes as permitted by the University and will not use confidential or sensitive data for personal use .
perturbation.changed_text: I may access confidential or sensitive data for any purpose, personal or otherwise, with the understanding that such access is subject to periodic review. I will not be held liable for accidental data disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original restriction on accessing data only for legitimate business purposes. By permitting access for any purpose, it creates a contradiction and removes the intended safeguard against misuse. Also the statement that, the person, won't be held liable for accidental data disclosure, makes that entire section and agreement moot.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I will not leave confidential or sensitive data in view of others who do not have a legitimate business reason to view the data and I will only share confidential data with persons having authorized access .
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows access to sensitive data for 'any purpose, personal or otherwise,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text's requirement that data is only shared with individuals having 'authorized access' and not left in view of others without a 'legitimate business reason.' The changed text removes the original restriction and introduces a conflicting allowance, creating a strong in-text contradiction as it breaks compatibility with the intended safeguard.

perturbation.original_text: I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information and if uncertain about the appropriateness of who this information may be shared with in the course of my authorized access , I will discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce. I understand that even the most innocuous ﬁle can contain conﬁdential information.
perturbation.changed_text: I am not responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of this information. All information is considered public domain unless explicitly marked otherwise by the data owner. It is not my responsibility to determine the appropriateness of sharing information; it is the responsibility of the data owner to inform me of any restrictions.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely negates the user's responsibility to maintain confidentiality, placing the burden solely on the data owner. This creates a contradiction with the overarching purpose of the confidentiality agreement and shifts the onus of protection. Moreover, in Section C, the user is responsible for misuse, or sensitive data, and now this is not the case.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I understand that misuse of confidential or sensitive information or data, whether intentional or due to neglect on my part, is a breach of policy and grounds for corrective action which may include my dismissal , the termination of access to University resources, or the termination of a contractual agreement and may subject me to possible civil and/or criminal legal action .
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the user is 'not responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of this information,' while the contradicted text holds the user accountable for 'misuse of confidential or sensitive information or data, whether intentional or due to neglect.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, as the user cannot be held responsible for something they are explicitly stated not to be responsible for. The changed text completely negates the user's responsibility, directly contradicting the section that holds them accountable for misuse.

perturbation.original_text: In all instances as described above, I acknowledge my personal obligation to protect, through execution of reasonable steps, the confidentialit y, integrity and privacy of this data in accordance with University policies, regulatory requirements or other special handling requirements, and prevent unauthorized access, modification, destruction or disclosure of sensitive or confidential data . In the absence of stated guidance or without a clear indication of sensitivity, I will treat and handle all data as if it were sensitive.
perturbation.changed_text: I have no personal obligation to protect the confidentiality, integrity, or privacy of this data. The University is solely responsible for implementing and enforcing security measures. I am only responsible for following explicitly stated instructions, and I am not required to treat data as sensitive in the absence of clear guidance. Any loss of data, is not to be held by the user, as they are not responsible.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the user's personal obligation to protect data, directly contradicting the preceding sections that emphasize their responsibility. By stating that the University is solely responsible, it creates uncertainty about the user's role in data security and shifts the entire burden to the institution. This section goes against all the Non-disclosure and Confidentiality clauses in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I will only access confidential or sensitive data for legitimate business purposes as permitted by the University and will not use confidential or sensitive data for personal use .
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly removes the user's personal obligation to protect data, while the contradicted text states that the user will only access data for legitimate business purposes. The changed text makes the University solely responsible, creating a direct conflict with the user's stated obligation in the contradicted text to only access data for business purposes. This is because the user is no longer responsible for any data breaches or misuse, which is a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I will not share m y computer user login IDs and passwords with anyone, at any time, for any reason, except in cases necessary to facilitate computer maintenance and repairs.
perturbation.changed_text: I may share my computer user login IDs and passwords with colleagues or supervisors if it facilitates workflow or collaboration. The responsibility for any misuse of shared credentials lies solely with the University's IT department.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the prohibition against sharing login credentials. By permitting sharing for workflow reasons, it undermines the security measures intended to prevent unauthorized access and creates a loophole for potential misuse. Furthermore, shifting the responsibility of misuse to the IT department alleviates liability of the person using the account, making the agreement useless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I will contact the University immediately if I discover that my computer user login ID or password has been compromised, if I suspect that University digital asset ha s been misused, or if I suspect that someone has obtained unauthorized access to confidential or sensitive information or data on the University of Nebraska information system .
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibits sharing login credentials, while the changed text permits sharing for workflow or collaboration. This directly contradicts the original intention of preventing unauthorized access. The contradicted text emphasizes the user's responsibility to report compromised credentials or suspected misuse, which is undermined by the changed text's permission to share credentials and shift the responsibility for misuse to the IT department. This creates a clear and direct conflict, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: I also understand that improper use of my computer user login ID or password is a breach of policy and grounds for corrective action which may include dismissal, the termination of access to University resources, or the termination of a contractual agreement and may subject me to possible civil and/or criminal legal action .
perturbation.changed_text: I am not liable for any use, proper or improper, of my computer user login ID or password. The University assumes all responsibility for monitoring and securing computer systems. The individual is not liable for action taken as a result of someone using their password.
perturbation.explanation: This completely removes the accountability clause for improper use of credentials, contradicting the entire premise of the computer access agreement. This change means any use of the account, even if nefarious, is not the fault of the user. In addition to rendering that, accountability moot, it introduces a lot of contradictions with the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is obligated to keep such data confidential .
perturbation.justification: The changed text states the user is 'not liable for any use, proper or improper, of my computer user login ID or password' and that 'the University assumes all responsibility'. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that 'every person who has been given access... is obligated to keep such data confidential'. The change removes user accountability, directly conflicting with the obligation to keep data confidential, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction, and aligning with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
